name: band vertical pallof press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and wrap the band around a sturdy object at chest height.
Hold the band with both hands and step away from the anchor point, creating tension in the band.
Position yourself perpendicular to the anchor point, with your side facing the band.
Extend your arms straight out in front of you, keeping your hands at chest height.
Engage your core and press the band away from your chest, fully extending your arms.
Hold the position for a few seconds, then slowly bring the band back towards your chest.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and wrap the band around a sturdy object at chest height. Hold the band with both hands and step away from the anchor point, creating tension in the band. Position yourself perpendicular to the anchor point, with your side facing the band. Extend your arms straight out in front of you, keeping your hands at chest height. Engage your core and press the band away from your chest, fully extending your arms. Hold the position for a few seconds, then slowly bring the band back towards your chest. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Avoid loaded spinal flexion and excessive hinge depth. Brace core and use supported positions.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
